American Psycho follows Patrick Bateman, a wealthy New York City investment banker who hides a dark, violent double life as a serial killer beneath his meticulously crafted, superficial yuppie persona. As he obsessively navigates a shallow corporate world of designer suits and expensive business cards, his sanity rapidly deteriorates into a series of graphic, surreal hallucinations and bloody rampages.

What makes the performance so legendary is Christian Baleâs extreme dedication to becoming Bateman. Bale was warned by industry insiders that taking the role would be career suicide, but he committed entirely, undergoing a brutal, multi-hour daily skincare and workout routine. He even stayed in character between takes, maintaining a fake, high-society American accent throughout the entire shoot, which genuinely unsettled the cast and crew.

Beyond television, Toler has authored multiple books about emotional intelligence, communication, marriage and relationships, using real-world wisdom to help people navigate lifeâs toughest conversations. Euphoria follows Rue Bennett, a 17-year-old girl struggling with drug addiction after returning home from rehab. As she tries to navigate recovery, she finds herself constantly battling personal demons, mental health challenges, and the pressures of growing up. Her life changes when she meets Jules Vaughn, a confident and free-spirited newcomer to town, and the two form a deep connection that becomes one of the most important relationships in her life.
The series explores the lives of several teenagers as they deal with identity, friendship, family problems, social media, love, and the consequences of their choices. Each character faces unique struggles: Nate Jacobs wrestles with anger and control issues, Cassie Howard searches for love and validation, Maddy Perez navigates a toxic relationship, Kat Hernandez explores self-confidence, and Lexi Howard tries to find her own voice while observing the chaos around her.
As relationships become more complicated and secrets begin to surface, the characters are forced to confront difficult truths about themselves and the people they care about. Throughout the story, Rue serves as both the main character and narrator, offering insight into the emotional highs and lows experienced by the people around her.
The series is known for its intense emotional storytelling, striking visuals, and exploration of the challenges young people face while trying to understand who they are and where they belong.

Nashville style hot chicken sandwich ð 

Ingredients 

For the chicken:

2 lb chicken breast, pounded to even thickness
2 cups buttermilk
1/3 cup pickle juice
1/4 cup hot sauce 
Salt to taste
1 tsp black pepper 
1 tbsp garlic powder 
1 tsp white pepper
1 tsp MSG
1 tbsp onion powder
1 tbsp smoked paprika powder 
Oil for frying 

For the dry batter:

2 cups all purpose flour 
1/3 cup corn starch 
Salt to taste
1 tsp black pepper 
1 tbsp garlic powder 
1 tbsp onion powder
1 tsp white pepper
1 tsp MSG
1 tbsp smoked paprika powder 

For the ranch dressing:
1/2 cup mayonnaise 
1/2 cup sour cream
1/2 cup butter milk
Salt and pepper to taste 
1 tbsp dilll
1 tbsp parsley 
1 tbsp garlic powder 
1 clove garlic, minced
1â2 tsp pickle juice

For the Nashville hot oil

1 tsp smoked paprika powder 
2â3 tbsp cayenne pepper
1 tsp chili powder
1 tbsp garlic powder 
1 cup hot frying oil
1 tbsp Brown Sugar
1 tsp Black pepper 
1/2 tsp Kosher Salt

For the creamy lettuce:

1 cup lettuce 
1/4 cup thinly sliced cabbage
2-3 tbsp ranch dressing 

To assemble:

6 brioche buns
1/2 cup pickles 
Butter for toasting buns

Method:

Combine buttermilk, pickle juice, hot sauce, salt, black/white pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, smoked paprika, and MSG. Add chicken, coat well, and marinate 4 hours or overnight.
Flour mixture: Mix flour, cornstarch, salt, black/white pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, smoked paprika, and MSG.

Ranch dressing: Whisk mayo, sour cream, buttermilk, dill, parsley, garlic, pickle juice, salt, and pepper for ranch; chill.

Remove the chicken from the marinade and coat in the flour mixture. Dip back into the marinade, then coat again for a double dredge. Let rest for 10 minutes before frying.

Fry at 350Â°F for 5â7 min per side until golden and 165Â°F internal.

Nashville hot oil: Mix smoked paprika, cayenne, chili powder, garlic powder, brown sugar, black pepper, and salt. Stir in hot frying oil and brush over chicken.

Toss lettuce + cabbage with ranch. Toast buns, assemble with pickles, and serve warm.

At 13, his dad went to jail on drug charges and his older brother was killed. Rocky, his mom, and sister ended up in shelters in NYC. He started selling crack and weed around 15th St in Harlem to get by. He talks about it in Peso and Purple Swag.

Reels have become one of the platformâs main content formats because they encourage high engagement and easy sharing. Businesses, influencers, and everyday users use Reels to build visibility, promote products, tell stories, or participate in viral trends. share.share_text:
Interesting fact:

Variable naming is one of those skills that separates engineers who write code for the computer from engineers who write code for the next human who has to read it at 2am during a production incident, and the gap between good and bad naming is almost never about intelligence but about the discipline of pausing long enough to ask whether the name communicates intent or just describes implementation. The classic antipatterns are so universal they have become industry jokes: single letter variables outside of a two line loop, names like data, info, temp, and result that describe the type of thing rather than what the thing actually represents, boolean variables named flag or status that tell you nothing about what condition they are tracking, and the particularly dangerous pattern of abbreviated names that made sense to the person who wrote them and read like a foreign language to everyone else six months later. The positive patterns that experienced engineers converge on share a common principle of naming things at the level of abstraction where they live: a variable in a business logic layer should read like a business concept, a variable in a utility function should read like the operation it supports, and the name should be long enough to be unambiguous and short enough that reading it does not feel like parsing a sentence. The most insidious naming antipattern is the one that is technically accurate but misleading in context, where a variable called userList actually contains only active premium users who have verified their email in the last thirty days, because the delta between the name and the reality is exactly the kind of assumption that produces bugs that take three engineers two days to find.

Have you ever spent more time deciphering what a variable name meant than it would have taken to rewrite the entire function it lived in?

Madison Beer was actually discovered in a very unexpected way. When she was just 13, she posted a cover of an Etta James song on YouTube, and it caught the attention of Justin Bieber, who shared it with his massive fanbase. That single moment instantly changed her life, bringing her millions of followers overnight and launching her into the music industry.

What makes it even more interesting is that despite that early boost, her path was far from smooth. She struggled with label issues and was even dropped early in her career, which forced her to rebuild everything independently. Instead of fading away, she used that setback to develop her own sound and identity.

By the time she released her debut album Life Support, she had already gone through years of industry challenges, making her success feel more earned than overnight.
